title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence · Compiler Case: Context Isolation
summary: Every page launches a browser, shares cookies and storage, waits for network idle, captures unstable DOM, and leaves contexts or downloads alive. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts context isolation increment in isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.
Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Context Isolation model · Theory Context Isolati…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Context Isolation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Context Isolation model · Theory Context Isolati…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5099 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Context Isolation model · Theory Context Isolati…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Opening a new Page fully isolates cookies, cache, permissions, and workers.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-context-isolation-32xjbm.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-context-isolation-32xjbm(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_context_isolation_32xjbm\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?browserCases < 12)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?closedContexts < 12 \|\| closedContexts > browserCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?readinessChanges < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?policyEscapes > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
